_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Perform secure program testing, review, and/or assessment to identify potential flaws in codes and mitigate vulnerabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test coverage, performance implications, documentation completeness, and adherence to team coding standards.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-009 (SP, cosine: 0.532), DQ-T-013 (DQ, cosine: 0.476).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.635). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [assessment, codes, flaws, identify, mitigate, perform] vs WIDAI-only terms [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.871 (P=0.878, R=0.863). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Perform secure program testing, review, and/or assessment to identify potential" and "Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.570 < STS 0.635 (gap -0.065). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [assessment, codes, flaws] and [adherence, application, changes] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [assessment, codes, flaws, identify, mitigate, perform]. WIDAI terms: [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness].
